steps.title: Recognize what no solution means
steps.content: A system of linear equations has no solution when the two lines are parallel. Parallel lines have equal slopes but different y-intercepts, so they never intersect.

steps.title: Compare slopes to find c
steps.content: Both equations are in slope-intercept form $y=mx+b$. The first line has slope $\frac{1}{2}$ and the second has slope $c$. For no solution, the slopes must match while the $y$-intercepts differ. The intercepts are already different, $8\ne10$, so set $c=\frac{1}{2}$. A one-row Desmos regression can match the slopes directly.
